response.justification: The candidate does not meet the fundamental requirement of 8+ years developing production-grade AI solutions. While they demonstrate strong general software engineering expertise and are currently working on relevant technologies at re:cinq (MCP, agentic workflows), their AI experience is approximately 1 year—far short of the required threshold. The resume lacks evidence of critical required technologies including Python, vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, and multi-agent frameworks. This is a staff-level role that demands deep, proven AI systems expertise; the candidate shows promise for growth but is currently overqualified in breadth and underqualified in depth.
strengths: Currently building relevant AI infrastructure: 1+ year at re:cinq working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, directly aligned with job's core technologies
Extensive software engineering pedigree: 20 years of production experience at Staff level spanning full-stack, microservices at scale, cloud infrastructure, and DevOps practices
Cloud platform maturity: Demonstrated AWS infrastructure expertise (CDK, deployment, systems handling millions of requests/second) with TypeScript and modern tooling
concerns: Critical experience gap: Only ~1 year in AI-focused work versus required 8+ years developing production-grade AI solutions
Missing core AI technologies: No demonstrated experience with Python, vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI)
No evidence of AI systems maturity work: Resume lacks examples of evaluation pipelines, observability frameworks for AI quality assurance, or shipped production AI products

key_factors.factor: Missing required AI/ML technologies (Python, vector DB, RAG, LLM optimization)
Strong engineering foundation + current trajectory in AI/MCP work
